skill.name: generative-art
skill.description: Create interactive p5.js generative art artifacts with seeded randomness, parameter controls, and reproducible variations.
skill.summary: Build reproducible, interactive generative art with p5.js templates and seed-based exploration.
skill.author: Cam10001110101

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, scripts, and network patterns. Review found command execution and weak-cryptography alerts are false positives from markdown examples, JavaScript regex use, and generative-art terminology; the only confirmed issue is expected browser loading of p5.js and Google Fonts from public CDNs.

low_findings.title: External Browser Assets Loaded From Public CDNs
low_findings.description: The skill instructs generated artifacts to load p5.js from cdnjs and the viewer template loads p5.js plus Google Fonts. This is normal for an interactive browser art template, but it creates a third-party availability and privacy dependency when the artifact is opened.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines directly load remote browser resources. The URLs are public library and font CDNs, with no evidence of credential collection or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Command-Execution Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported README and SKILL.md locations are markdown code fences, file tree examples, or instructions to read local templates. The JavaScript exec hit is RegExp.prototype.exec used to parse a hex color, not process execution or dynamic code execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed contexts show documentation examples and a regular-expression method call. No shell, child process, eval, or user-controlled command invocation was found.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged weak-cryptography locations describe seeded randomness, algorithmic art, seed navigation, or responsive design comments. They do not implement MD5, SHA-1, encryption, password hashing, or security-sensitive randomness.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected text uses art and randomness terminology rather than cryptographic primitives. There is no security context where weak cryptography would affect users.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Benign Template and UI References
low_findings.description: System and network reconnaissance hits point to browser layout, canvas, parameter controls, and local skill file references. No code enumerates system information, scans networks, reads environment variables, or collects host data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed lines are static documentation, CSS, or art-template comments. No semantic evidence of host or network reconnaissance was found.

content.value_statement: Creative coding can be slow when every sketch starts from an empty file. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to produce p5.js art with reusable controls, seeds, and export features.
actual_capabilities: Creates an algorithmic art philosophy before implementation.
Uses a p5.js HTML viewer template with sidebar controls.
Supports seeded randomness for reproducible visual variations.
Defines adjustable parameters such as counts, scales, speeds, and colors.
Includes seed navigation, regeneration, reset, and PNG download controls.
Provides JavaScript reference patterns for particles, noise, palettes, and export.
limitations: Generated artifacts depend on browser JavaScript and p5.js.
The included viewer loads p5.js and fonts from external CDNs.
It is a template-guided workflow, not a full visual design application.
Complex sketches may need performance tuning for older devices.

use_cases.title: Prototype Interactive Art Sketches
use_cases.description: Create a browser-ready p5.js sketch with controls for fast exploration.

use_cases.title: Generate Reproducible Design Variations
use_cases.description: Use seeds and parameters to compare consistent visual outputs.

use_cases.title: Teach Algorithmic Art Concepts
use_cases.description: Show how particles, noise, recursion, and parameters shape visual systems.

prompt_templates.title: Start A Simple Sketch
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one interactive artwork.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a p5.js generative art artifact inspired by wind patterns. Include seeded randomness, three useful controls, and PNG download.

prompt_templates.title: Explore A Visual System
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an interactive generative artwork based on wave interference. Make the seed, frequency, amplitude, and palette adjustable.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Series
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Art Blocks style generative system with seeds 1 through 20. Explain which parameters control composition, color, and density.

prompt_templates.title: Design A Custom Algorithm
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a distinct computational aesthetic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Develop an algorithmic philosophy for urban growth, then implement it as a self-contained p5.js artifact with optimized controls and export.

output_examples.input: Create generative art inspired by ocean currents.
output_examples.output: A self-contained interactive HTML artwork with a flow-based p5.js sketch, seed controls, wave-density sliders, palette controls, and PNG export.

output_examples.input: Make a reproducible series of abstract botanical forms.
output_examples.output: An algorithmic philosophy for recursive growth plus an interactive viewer where each seed produces a consistent branching composition.

output_examples.input: Help me teach seeded randomness in creative coding.
output_examples.output: A classroom-ready p5.js example with simple controls, visible seed navigation, and a short explanation of how parameters affect the artwork.

best_practices: Start from the included viewer template so seed controls and export behavior remain consistent.
Keep every important visual choice connected to a named parameter or seed.
Test high particle counts and animation speed before sharing the artifact.
anti_patterns: Do not create a static image when the request needs an interactive generative system.
Do not use unbounded loops or extreme element counts without performance limits.
Do not add extra remote scripts beyond the required p5.js dependency.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates interactive p5.js generative art artifacts with controls for seeds, parameters, colors, regeneration, and export.

faq.question: Can outputs be reproduced?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill emphasizes seeded randomness, so the same seed should recreate the same visual result.

faq.question: Does it require a server?
faq.answer: No. The intended output is a single HTML artifact that runs in a browser or Claude artifact view.

faq.question: Does it load external resources?
faq.answer: Yes. The provided viewer template loads p5.js and Google Fonts from public CDNs.

faq.question: Can users download the artwork?
faq.answer: Yes. The template includes a PNG download action for the current canvas.

faq.question: Is this only for particle systems?
faq.answer: No. The templates mention particles, recursion, wave systems, circle packing, Voronoi patterns, and other algorithmic approaches.
